items.title_en: Sefa Utaki: Supreme Sacred Site of the Ryukyu Kingdom
items.title_ja: 斎場御嶽：琉球王国最高聖地
items.desc_en: The most sacred site in the Ryukyu Kingdom, located in Nanjo City. Used for the coronation ceremony (oaraori) of the Kikoe-ogimi (supreme priestess). Contains six ibi (sacred spaces) including the Sangui, a triangular rock formation opening toward Kudaka Island. UNESCO World Heritage Site since 2000
items.desc_ja: 南城市に位置する琉球王国最高の聖地。聞得大君の就任儀礼「御新下り」が行われた。三庫理をはじめ6つの拝所を持ち、久高島を望む。2000年世界遺産登録。

items.title_en: Kudaka Island: Island of the Gods and Origin of Agriculture
items.title_ja: 久高島：神の島と農耕発祥の地
items.desc_en: Small island east of the Okinawa mainland considered the most sacred place in Ryukyuan cosmology. Believed to be where the deity Amamikiyo descended from heaven to create the Ryukyu Islands. The Izaiho ceremony (last held 1978) was a 12-yearly initiation of priestesses. Origin myth of rice and grain
items.desc_ja: 沖縄本島東方の小島。琉球神話でアマミキヨが天から降り立った聖地。イザイホー（最終催行1978年）は12年に一度の神女就任儀礼。五穀発祥伝承の地。

items.title_ja: ニライカナイ：海の彼方の理想郷
items.desc_en: Central concept in Ryukyuan cosmology referring to a paradise beyond the sea from which deities, ancestors, and blessings originate. Prayers at coastal utaki are directed toward nirai kanai. The concept shapes funeral orientation, agricultural prayers, and the spiritual geography of village layouts.
items.desc_ja: 琉球世界観の中核概念。海の彼方にある神々と祖先の楽土。沿岸の御嶽での祈りはニライカナイに向けられる。葬送・農耕祈願・集落配置の精神的地理を規定。

items.title_ja: ウマチー：集落農耕儀礼
items.desc_en: Three annual agricultural festivals (February, March, and June by lunar calendar) conducted at village utaki. Involves offering of first fruits, prayers for abundant harvest, and communal feasting. The noro priestess leads the ceremonies while village men prepare offerings.
items.desc_ja: 旧暦2月・3月・6月に御嶽で行われる3回の農耕儀礼。初穂の奉納、豊穣祈願、共同饗宴を含む。ノロが祭祀を主導し、男性が供物を準備する。

items.title_en: Ungami Festival: Sea God Festival and Maritime Prayers
items.title_ja: ウンガミ祭：海神祭と海の祈り
items.desc_en: Annual festival honoring sea deities, held in coastal villages during the 7th month of the lunar calendar. Features dragon boat races (haarii), prayers for safe voyages and abundant catches. The noro leads prayers at the shore, invoking nirai kanai for the community's maritime prosperity.
items.desc_ja: 旧暦7月に沿岸集落で行われる海神を祀る年中行事。爬龍船競漕、航海安全・豊漁祈願を含む。ノロが浜辺でニライカナイに向けて祈願を主導する。

items.title_en: Shiimi: Qingming Tomb Visiting and Ancestor Veneration
items.title_ja: シーミー（清明祭）：墓参と祖先崇拝
items.desc_en: Okinawan adaptation of the Chinese Qingming festival, held in April. Families gather at munchuu (lineage) tombs for picnics with ancestors, offering uchinsaku (重箱料理) containing 9 dishes. The event reinforces patrilineal kinship bonds and ancestral connections across generations.
items.desc_ja: 中国の清明節を沖縄化した4月の行事。門中墓に親族が集い、重箱料理（ウチンサク）9品を供え祖先と食事する。父系親族の絆と世代間の祖先接続を強化する。

items.title_en: Hinukan: Hearth Deity and Household Spirituality
items.title_ja: ヒヌカン（火の神）：竈の神と家庭霊性
items.desc_en: The hearth deity worshipped in every traditional Okinawan household. The female head of household maintains the hinukan altar near the kitchen, offering incense and prayers on the 1st and 15th of each lunar month. Hinukan mediates between the household and the village utaki system.
items.desc_ja: 全ての伝統的沖縄家庭で祀られる竈の神。女性家長が台所近くの香炉を管理し、旧暦1日・15日に線香を供える。家庭と集落御嶽体系を媒介する。

items.title_en: Haarii Dragon Boat Race: Maritime Ritual Competition
items.title_ja: ハーリー（爬龍船競漕）：海の儀礼競争
items.desc_en: Dragon boat racing held annually in fishing villages, especially prominent in Naha (May 4th lunar calendar) and Itoman. Three boats representing different community groups race to ensure abundant fishing and safe seas. Combines Chinese dragon boat tradition with Ryukyuan maritime spirituality.
items.desc_ja: 漁業集落で年1回開催される爬龍船競漕。那覇（旧暦5月4日）・糸満で盛大。3隻の舟が豊漁と航海安全を祈って競う。中国龍舟と琉球海洋霊性の融合。

items.title_en: Tsunahiki: Great Tug of War Ritual
items.title_ja: 綱引き：大綱挽き儀礼
items.desc_en: Massive tug-of-war events held across Okinawa, most famously in Naha (October) with a 200m, 40-ton rope. The east (agi) and west (iri) sides represent cosmic dualities. The rope's interlocking male and female ends symbolize fertility. Winners predict agricultural fortune for the coming year.
items.desc_ja: 沖縄各地で行われる大綱引き。那覇（10月）は全長200m・40トンの綱で最大級。東（アギ）と西（イリ）が宇宙的二元性を表す。綱の雌雄結合が豊穣を象徴。

items.title_en: Obotsu Kagura: Celestial Realm in Ryukyuan Cosmology
items.title_ja: オボツカグラ：琉球世界観の天上界
items.desc_en: The celestial counterpart to nirai kanai in Ryukyuan cosmology. While nirai kanai lies beyond the sea, obotsu kagura represents the heavenly realm where deities reside. The dual structure of above/below, mountain/sea shapes the spiritual geography of utaki placement and prayer orientation.
items.desc_ja: ニライカナイの天上対応概念。海の彼方がニライカナイなら、天上界がオボツカグラ。上下・山海の二重構造が御嶽配置と祈りの方向性を規定する琉球宇宙論。

items.title_ja: 御嶽浄化儀式
items.desc_en: Sacred protocols for purifying utaki groves before major ceremonies. Practitioners perform water ablutions, salt offerings, and verbal incantations to prepare spiritual spaces and remove spiritual pollution from the sacred landscape.
items.desc_ja: 大祭前の御嶽浄化儀式。水による禊、塩供え、言葉による清めで霊的汚れを除去し聖域を整える伝統的儀礼。

items.title_ja: ノロ世襲制
items.desc_en: Hereditary system of female noro priestesses serving utaki sanctuaries. Daughters inherit sacred duties, ritual knowledge, and spiritual authority from mothers through family lineage, maintaining continuous spiritual guardianship of sacred groves.
items.desc_ja: 母から娘へ継承される女性神官制度。御嶽の霊的守護者として代々受け継がれる聖なる職務と儀礼知識。

items.title_ja: ハーリー共同儀式
items.desc_en: Traditional dragon boat racing ceremony combining religious worship with communal labor. Teams from different districts compete during festival celebrations, honoring ocean deities and celebrating maritime heritage through synchronized rowing and spiritual invocation.
items.desc_ja: 竜舟競争を通じた地域共同の宗教儀式。海の神々への礼拝と同期した漕ぎで海洋伝統を祝う祭礼。

items.title_ja: 綱引き宇宙観
items.desc_en: Spiritual cosmology embedded in tsunahiki rope-pulling contests representing cosmic balance between opposing forces. The ritual demonstrates terrestrial harmony with celestial order through synchronized community participation and symbolic struggle imagery.
items.desc_ja: 綱引きに込められた宇宙的均衡思想。対立する力のバランスを象徴する儀式で天地秩序との調和を表現。

items.title_ja: ニライカナイ航海信仰
items.desc_en: Navigational spirituality referencing the mythological paradise island Nirai-Kanai located beyond the horizon. Fishermen and sailors observe traditional directional practices and protective rituals when traveling toward sacred ocean realms.
items.desc_ja: 地平線の彼方の理想郷ニライカナイへの航海信仰。漁師による方位観察と海上での保護儀式。

items.title_ja: 御嶽境界標識
items.desc_en: Traditional methods of marking utaki sacred grove boundaries using natural materials and spiritual symbols. Practitioners place sacred stones, ritual ropes, and symbolic markers to delineate holy spaces and protect them from secular intrusion.
items.desc_ja: 石、縄、シンボルで御嶽の聖域を画定する伝統的方法。俗界からの侵入防止と聖域保護。

items.title_ja: 季節的御嶽参拝暦
items.desc_en: Elaborate ritual calendar prescribing specific visitation times to different utaki sanctuaries throughout the year. Communities observe precise seasonal sequences of prayers and offerings synchronized with agricultural cycles and celestial observations.
items.desc_ja: 年間を通じた御嶽参拝の厳密な時系列。農業周期と天体観察に同期した祈りと供え。

items.title_ja: ハーリー神呼び唱え
items.desc_en: Specialized vocal chanting performed during haarii boat races to invoke ocean deities and marine spirits. Rhythmic incantations synchronized with rowing motions create spiritual communion with water realms while energizing competitive team performance.
items.desc_ja: ハーリー中の海神を呼ぶ唱え。漕ぎのリズムに同期した詞で海の精霊との霊的交感を造成。

items.title_ja: 綱引き性別補完的役割
items.desc_en: Tug-of-war ceremony employing distinct gender roles where men and women occupy separate rope sections with complementary spiritual functions. The dual-gender structure represents cosmological duality and maintains social harmony through prescribed participation patterns.
items.desc_ja: 男女が異なる綱の位置を占める補完的役割分担。二元的宇宙観と社会調和を表現する構造。

items.title_ja: ニライカナイ贈与相互性
items.desc_en: Reciprocal exchange system with the mythological Nirai-Kanai realm where offerings are given expecting spiritual blessings in return. Communities perform ritualized gift-giving to invisible divine entities maintaining cosmic reciprocity and seasonal abundance.
items.desc_ja: 理想郷への供え物と霊的祝福の相互交換制度。宇宙的相互性と季節的豊穣を維持する。

items.title_ja: 御嶽の樹冠浄化儀礼
items.desc_en: Sacred grove canopy purification rituals performed to maintain spiritual sanctity. Practitioners climb ancient trees within utaki sites, conducting offerings and prayers to strengthen celestial connections and ensure forest vitality.
items.desc_ja: 御嶽内の樹冠を浄化する儀礼。古木に登り、天との結びつきを強め、森の活力を保つための供え物と祈りを捧げる。

items.title_ja: ニライカナイ方位航法
items.desc_en: Traditional navigation knowledge using nirai-kanai directional markers. Fishermen and maritime specialists employ sacred directions toward the otherworldly realm to guide fishing expeditions and ensure safe passage.
items.desc_ja: ニライカナイの方位を用いた航海知識。漁師が他界への聖なる方向を使い、漁へと導き安全な航海を保証する。

items.title_ja: ハーリー豊収祈願儀式
items.desc_en: Communal haarii rope-pulling rituals combining physical collective action with spiritual invocation. Teams pull sacred ropes while chanting prayers, invoking bountiful harvests and community prosperity through synchronized effort.
items.desc_ja: 綱引きハーリーと精神的祈願を組み合わせた儀式。チームが聖なる綱を引きながら、豊かな収穫と共同体の繁栄を祈る。

items.title_ja: 綱引き性別役割分担
items.desc_en: Tsunahiki tug-of-war with gender-complementary roles embodying cosmic balance. Male and female teams represent opposing sacred forces; their struggle symbolizes seasonal transitions and agricultural renewal through complementary opposition.
items.desc_ja: 男女の役割分担による綱引き。相対する聖なる力を表現し、季節の転換と農業の更新を象徴する。

items.title_ja: 御嶽での先祖霊との交通
items.desc_en: Ritual communication with ancestral spirits within utaki sacred spaces. Female priestesses facilitate dialogue between living communities and deceased forebears, maintaining genealogical continuity and spiritual protection.
items.desc_ja: 御嶽での先祖霊との対話儀礼。女性聖職者が生者と故人の交通を仲介し、系統継続性と精神的保護を保つ。

items.title_ja: 御嶽の神聖植物知識
items.desc_en: Specialized knowledge of medicinal and ritual plants within utaki groves. Practitioners identify healing herbs and sacred vegetation for ceremonial use, maintaining ecological and spiritual balance within protected forest spaces.
items.desc_ja: 御嶽内の薬用植物と儀礼植物の知識。癒しの草や聖なる植物を特定し、保護された森の生態と精神的バランスを保つ。

items.title_ja: 儀礼暦の太陽観測法
items.desc_en: Astronomical observation methods determining ceremonial timing. Elders track solar positions relative to utaki landmarks, calculating seasonal ceremonies and agricultural festivals aligned with celestial cycles.
items.desc_ja: 御嶽を基準点とした太陽位置観測。年長者が季節儀礼と農業祭祀の時期を決定し、天体周期と整合させる。

items.title_ja: ハーリー掛け声調整技法
items.desc_en: Specialized chanting protocols coordinating haarii rope-pulling teams. Leaders employ rhythmic vocalizations synchronizing collective force, embedding prayers and community identity within participatory soundscapes.
items.desc_ja: ハーリーチーム調整の掛け声技法。指導者がリズム的な声で集団力を同期させ、祈りと共同体アイデンティティを組み込む。

items.title_ja: ニライカナイからの季節来訪期待
items.desc_en: Interpretive knowledge of seasonal resource cycles originating from nirai-kanai realm. Communities anticipate fish runs, wind patterns, and plant growth based on sacred directional beliefs and observational experience.
items.desc_ja: ニライカナイ由来の季節資源循環の解釈。魚群、風向、植生成長を聖なる信念と観察経験で予測する。

items.title_ja: 綱引き勝敗象徴解釈
items.desc_en: Interpretive frameworks assigning cosmic significance to tsunahiki tug-of-war outcomes. Victory or defeat symbolizes agricultural success, weather patterns, and spiritual balance; elders explain results to community members.
items.desc_ja: 綱引きの勝敗を宇宙的意味として解釈する知識体系。勝敗は農業成功、気象、精神的バランスを象徴する。

items.title_ja: 竹富島の種取祭
items.desc_en: Ritual festival celebrating successful seed harvesting and agricultural prosperity. Features traditional prayers, ceremonial dances, and community thanksgiving rituals ensuring future crop abundance and expressing gratitude to deities.
items.desc_ja: 種子の収穫成功と農業繁栄を祝う儀式祭。伝統的な祈り、儀式舞踊、地域感謝の儀式を特徴とし、将来の豊作を確保し神への感謝を表現。

items.title_ja: 宮古島パーントゥ
items.desc_en: Masked festival ritual performed during New Year celebrations where performers wearing mud-covered masks playfully bless homes and fields. Represents ancient fertility rites and purification practices ensuring prosperity and protection.
items.desc_ja: 正月の祝祭の際に土が覆われた仮面を被った演者が家と田畑を楽しく祝福するマスク祭儀。古代の豊穣儀式と清浄化の実践を表現し、繁栄と保護を確保する。

items.title_ja: 糸満大綱引き
items.desc_en: Historic community event featuring enormous handmade rope where thousands participate in ritual tug-of-war. Symbolizes unity, prosperity, and supernatural protection, with celebrations and traditional performances accompanying the main event.
items.desc_ja: 数千人が参加する巨大な手作りロープを用いた儀式的綱引きを特徴とする歴史的な地域行事。統一、繁栄、超自然的保護を象徴し、祝賀と伝統的パフォーマンスが随伴される。

items.title_ja: 沖縄本島のシヌグ
items.desc_en: Ancient agricultural ritual performed in spring to ensure bountiful crops and purify fields. Involves ceremonial processions, traditional offerings, and spiritual cleansing practices connecting farmers to land deities and natural cycles.
items.desc_ja: 豊作を確保し田畑を清浄化するために春に行われる古代的な農業儀式。儀式的な行進、伝統的供物、農民を土地神と自然の循環に結ぶ精神的清浄化の実践を含む。

items.title_ja: 首里城復元技術
items.desc_en: Specialized techniques for reconstructing historical castle structures using traditional materials, architectural knowledge, and archaeological evidence to authentically restore the symbolic seat of Ryukyu Kingdom.
items.desc_ja: 歴史的建造物の忠実な復元に必要とされる技術。伝統材料と考古学的知見を組み合わせた琉球王国の象徴的復元。

items.title_ja: 御嶽の石垣修復技術
items.desc_en: Specialized techniques for restoring stone structures at sacred worship sites using dry-stacking methods and traditional knowledge, maintaining spiritual sanctity and structural integrity of Okinawan sacred spaces.
items.desc_ja: 聖地の石積み構造物を修復する伝統技法。乾積み工法により宗教的聖性と構造的安定性を両立させる。

items.title_en: Munchuu System: Patrilineal Descent Groups and Genealogy
items.title_ja: 門中制度：父系出自集団と系図
items.desc_en: Patrilineal kinship organization (munchuu) that structures Okinawan social life. Each munchuu maintains genealogical records, shares an ancestral tomb, and conducts collective rituals (especially Shiimi/Qingming). The system defines inheritance, marriage eligibility, and mutual aid obligations. Firs
items.desc_ja: 沖縄社会を構造化する父系親族組織。各門中は系図を維持し、先祖の墓を共有し、シーミー等の集合的祭祀を行う。相続・婚姻適格・相互扶助義務を規定。長男相続が伝統。

items.title_en: Moai: Rotating Credit Association and Mutual Aid
items.title_ja: 模合（モアイ）：頼母子講と相互扶助
items.desc_en: Traditional rotating savings and credit association where members contribute a fixed amount monthly and take turns receiving the pooled sum. Functions as both financial instrument and social bonding mechanism. Modern moai groups may focus on drinking/socializing rather than financial needs. Reflects
items.desc_ja: 月例で固定額を出し合い、順番に総額を受け取る伝統的頼母子講。金融手段であると同時に社会的結合装置として機能。現代では飲み会・親睦目的の模合も多い。ゆいまーる精神を反映。

items.title_en: Yuimaru: Reciprocal Labor Exchange and Community Cooperation
items.title_ja: ゆいまーる：相互扶助労働交換
items.desc_en: Traditional system of reciprocal labor exchange where community members help each other with harvests, house building, and celebrations without monetary compensation. The accumulated social debt creates networks of obligation that bind communities together. The concept extends to emotional support a
items.desc_ja: 収穫・建築・祝い事で金銭を介さず互いに労働を提供する相互扶助体系。蓄積された社会的負債が共同体を結ぶ義務のネットワークを形成。感情的支援や危機対応にも拡張される概念。

items.title_ja: 門中制度
items.desc_en: Extended family organization tracing patrilineal descent, managing collective property and obligations. Members share ancestor veneration, mutual aid, and ceremonial responsibilities across generations within structured hierarchical framework.
items.desc_ja: 父系血統に基づく拡大家族組織。共有財産管理と相互扶助義務を担い、祖先祭祀と儀式責任を世代間で継承する階層的構造。

items.title_ja: 模合制度
items.desc_en: Community-based rotating savings and credit association where members contribute fixed amounts monthly, with payouts distributed among participants sequentially. Strengthens economic cooperation and social bonds through mutual financial support mechanisms.
items.desc_ja: 成員が毎月定額を拠出し、順番に全額を受け取る相互扶助の金銭制度。経済協力と社会結合を強化し、村落単位で実践される。

items.title_ja: ゆいまーる
items.desc_en: Reciprocal labor cooperation system where community members assist each other in agricultural work, construction, and major household tasks without monetary compensation. Reinforces village solidarity and ensures resource redistribution across economic strata.
items.desc_ja: 農作業や建築、家事労働を無償で相互扶助する労働交換制度。村落共同体の結合を強化し、経済的困窮家庭を支援する仕組み。

items.title_ja: 島村落統治
items.desc_en: Traditional village administrative organization managing collective land use, resource distribution, and conflict resolution through consensus-based decision-making. Elders and community leaders maintain social order and enforce customary regulations.
items.desc_ja: 共有地利用と資源配分を管理し、合意形成に基づく紛争解決を行う村落行政。長老と指導者が慣習規範を維持する。

items.title_ja: 結婚式行列儀礼
items.desc_en: Formal ceremonial procession accompanying bride between munchuu residences, involving ritual music, symbolic dress, and community witnessing. Establishes social bonds between families and publicly legitimizes marital union within extended kinship networks.
items.desc_ja: 新婦を新郎家の門中へ導く儀礼的行列。音楽、装束、親族参加により社会的結合を確立し、婚姻を公式に認証する。

items.title_ja: 葬儀調整制度
items.desc_en: Munchuu-organized funeral arrangements coordinating mourning periods, ritualistic preparations, and commemorative obligations. Distributes ceremonial responsibilities among family members and manages ancestral veneration rites spanning multiple days.
items.desc_ja: 門中による葬儀組織。喪の期間、儀式準備、追悼義務を調整し、複数日間の祖先祭祀責任を配分する。

items.title_ja: 門中長子相続権
items.desc_en: Patrilineal inheritance system granting eldest son preferential access to family properties and ceremonial leadership roles. Maintains economic stability and preserves ritual authority within extended family lineages across generational succession.
items.desc_ja: 長男が家督と儀式指導権を相続する父系制。経済基盤を維持し、門中内の儀礼権限を世代継承する。

items.title_ja: 模合成員選定基準
items.desc_en: Community established protocols determining moai group membership based on economic reliability, social standing, and neighborhood residence. Ensures financial trustworthiness and strengthens localized community networks through deliberate participant selection.
items.desc_ja: 経済的信頼性と社会的地位に基づく模合成員の選定。近隣住民による信用構築で、地域結合を強化する基準。

items.title_ja: 島村落農地配分
items.desc_en: Village-level system allocating arable land plots to households based on family size, economic need, and historical usage rights. Prevents wealth concentration, ensures food security, and regulates resource access through collective stewardship principles.
items.desc_ja: 家族規模と経済需要に基づき農地を配分する村落制度。富の集中を防ぎ、食糧安全保障と資源平等利用を維持する。

items.title_ja: 葬儀模合協力金
items.desc_en: Specialized moai group established specifically for funeral expense mutual aid, allowing members to receive financial assistance during death-related ceremonial costs. Ensures families can conduct proper rituals without economic hardship.
items.desc_ja: 葬儀費用支援専門の模合。成員が葬儀時に経済援助を受け、儀式的負担を軽減し、円滑な追悼儀礼を実現する。

items.title_ja: 門中の系図管理
items.desc_en: Systematic documentation of family lineage within munchuu groups, recording genealogical relationships across generations. Eldest members maintain detailed records of births, marriages, and deaths to preserve family history and inheritance rights.
items.desc_ja: 門中内の家系図を系統的に記録・管理する慣行。出生、婚姻、死亡を記録し、複数世代にわたる家族関係を保存。相続権と家族の歴史を維持する。

items.title_ja: 模合の回転金融
items.desc_en: Monthly gathering where community members contribute equal amounts to a collective fund, with one member receiving the total amount each meeting. This informal rotating credit system supports major household expenses like weddings, home construction, and emergencies.
items.desc_ja: 毎月集合して等額を拠出し、回転的に総額を受け取る金融システム。結婚式、家屋建設、緊急費用などの大規模支出を相互に支援する。

items.title_ja: ゆいまるの労働交換
items.desc_en: Reciprocal labor-sharing arrangement where community members assist each other with agricultural work, construction, and household tasks without monetary compensation. Records of work exchanges ensure balanced participation and future obligation fulfillment.
items.desc_ja: 農作業、建設、家事などで相互に無償労働を提供する慣行。労働交換記録により均衡の取れた参加と将来の義務履行を確保する。

items.title_ja: 島村落の結婚式寄付制度
items.desc_en: Organized village practice where households contribute money and labor resources to support wedding ceremonies. Contributions are recorded and reciprocated when families later host their own weddings, ensuring community-wide economic participation.
items.desc_ja: 結婚式を支援するため村落内の各戸が金銭と労働を提供する制度。寄付は記録され、後に自家の結婚式時に報いられる。

items.title_ja: 葬儀における門中の集団責任
items.desc_en: Extended family members and munchuu groups share funeral responsibilities including body preparation, grave maintenance, and ritual observance. Leadership roles rotate among senior members, and cost-sharing ensures financial burden distribution.
items.desc_ja: 葬儀の準備、埋葬地管理、儀式执行を門中構成員が共同で担当。指導的役割は年長者間で交代し、経費は分散される。

items.title_ja: 模合への加入儀礼と選定
items.desc_en: Traditional selection criteria and initiation protocols for moai membership, ensuring participants are trustworthy and financially stable. Acceptance requires village elder approval and consensus, with formal ceremonies marking membership entrance.
items.desc_ja: 模合加入者の選定基準と入会儀礼。信頼性と経済的安定性を確認し、村落長老の承認と合意により加入。入会時に形式的な儀礼を実施。

items.title_ja: 門中内の紛争解決法
items.desc_en: Formal procedures for resolving disputes within extended family structures, using mediation by senior munchuu members. Emphasis on consensus-building, face-saving, and restoration of harmony rather than adversarial resolution.
items.desc_ja: 門中内の紛争を年長者の仲介により解決する手続き。合意形成、面目保全、調和の回復を重視し、対立的解決は避ける。

items.title_ja: 島村落の共同食事の運営
items.desc_en: Coordinated village practice organizing collective meals for major events and seasonal celebrations. Households take turns providing ingredients and labor, with menus adapted to available local resources and seasonal harvests.
items.desc_ja: 大きな行事と季節の祝いのため村落で共同食事を企画。各戸が順番に食材と労働を提供。地域の資源と季節の収穫に合わせた献立。

items.title_ja: ゆいまるによる子育て支援
items.desc_en: Collective childcare practice where village women cooperatively supervise and nurture children during community work periods. Shared responsibility reduces individual family burden while maintaining cultural transmission of values and social norms.
items.desc_ja: 村落の女性が協力して共同作業中に子どもを監督・養育する慣行。個別家庭の負担軽減と文化的価値の伝承を実現。

items.title_ja: 門中の祖先祭祀暦
items.desc_en: Coordinated annual schedule of ancestor worship ceremonies observed by extended family members. Regular observances include birth anniversaries of significant ancestors, seasonal festivals, and collective tomb visits strengthening munchuu unity.
items.desc_ja: 門中構成員が守る祖先祭祀の年間日程。著名な祖先の誕生記念日、季節祭、集団墓参により門中の結束を強化。

items.title_ja: 門中の当主継承儀礼
items.desc_en: Formal procedures for transferring munchuu leadership authority to designated heir. Involves ritual acknowledgment by family members, property documentation, and ancestral veneration ceremonies ensuring continuity of lineage responsibilities and social standing.
items.desc_ja: 門中の当主から次期指導者への権威移譲の儀式的手続き。家族成員の儀礼的認可、財産記録、祖先祭祀を含み、血統の責任と社会的地位の継続性を保証する。

items.title_ja: 模合の緊急食糧援助制度
items.desc_en: Mutual aid mechanism within moai rotating savings groups to provide emergency food supplies during natural disasters or family hardship. Members contribute surplus harvests and preserved foods into communal reserves distributed during crises.
items.desc_ja: 模合の相互扶助メカニズムで、災害や家族困窮時に食糧供給を提供。成員が余剰農産物と保存食を共同備蓄に寄与し、危機時に配分される。

items.title_en: Yuimaru Labor Exchange for House Construction
items.title_ja: 結いまるによる住宅建設労働交換
items.desc_en: Community-organized collective labor system where shima village residents pool workforce for house building projects. Participants trade working hours without monetary compensation, strengthening social bonds while reducing individual construction costs significantly.
items.desc_ja: 島の住民が住宅建設の労働力を集約する地域組織的なシステム。参加者が報酬なしで労働時間を交換し、社会結合を強化しつつ個別建設費用を大幅削減。

items.title_ja: 門中の祖先追悼暦
items.desc_en: Detailed genealogical calendar maintained by munchuu members documenting death anniversaries and memorial observance dates. Ensures all ancestors receive appropriate ritual remembrance, coordinating family gatherings for spiritual obligations across generations.
items.desc_ja: 門中成員により保管される詳細な世系暦。死亡忌日と追悼観察日を記録し、全祖先が適切な儀礼を受けることを保証し、世代間の精神的義務のための家族集会を調整。

items.title_ja: 模合の結婚式費用分担制度
items.desc_en: Rotating moai group members contribute fixed monetary amounts toward member wedding expenses. Contributions cycle through members, providing substantial financial support for elaborate traditional Okinawan wedding ceremonies and celebration preparations.
items.desc_ja: 模合成員が結婚式費用に定額を分担。寄与が成員間で循環し、精巧な沖縄伝統結婚式と祝賀準備のための実質的な財政支援を提供。

items.title_ja: 島村の葬儀調整ネットワーク
items.desc_en: Integrated village system organizing collective funeral responsibilities across households. Designates specific members for food preparation, grave maintenance, ritual coordination, and mourning period management, distributing labor equitably among all residents.
items.desc_ja: 世帯間の葬儀責任を組織する統合村システム。食事準備、墓地保守、儀礼調整、喪の期間管理の特定成員を指定し、全住民間で労働を公平に配分。

items.title_ja: 結いまるの季節農業労働班
items.desc_en: Flexible yuimaru teams organize rotating seasonal agricultural labor across village farmlands. Members work collectively on planting, weeding, and harvesting cycles, with task rotation ensuring equitable participation and knowledge transfer of farming techniques.
items.desc_ja: 柔軟な結いまるチームが農地間の季節農業労働を組織。成員が播種、除草、収穫サイクルで共同作業し、任務交換が公平な参加と農業技術知識の伝承を保証。

items.title_ja: 門中の結婚式儀礼専門家
items.desc_en: Designated munchuu elders serving as wedding ceremony experts, directing ritual sequences, managing guest seating arrangements, and ensuring proper ancestral acknowledgment. Knowledge transmitted through family lineage observation and direct instruction over decades.
items.desc_ja: 結婚式儀礼の専門家として機能する指定門中長老。儀式順序の指揮、客席配置の管理、適切な祖先認識を保証。知識は数十年間の家族血統観察と直接指導を通じて伝承。

items.title_ja: 模合の葬儀費用循環基金
items.desc_en: Moai members maintain rotating funeral fund reserves, providing significant financial assistance when member households experience deaths. Contributions standardized, distributions managed transparently, alleviating economic burden of expensive funeral preparations and ceremonies.
items.desc_ja: 模合成員が葬儀基金循環準備金を保持。成員世帯が死亡時に多額の財政援助を提供。寄付は標準化、配分は透明に管理され、高額な葬儀準備と儀礼の経済的負担を軽減。

items.title_ja: 島村の境界保守集団
items.desc_en: Organized village teams performing annual maintenance of communal land boundaries, irrigation systems, and shared resources. Yuimaru-based work coordination ensures infrastructure protection, dispute prevention, and collective responsibility for village territorial integrity.
items.desc_ja: 共同土地境界、灌漑システム、共有資源の年次保守を実施する組織化村チーム。結いまるベースの作業調整が基盤保護、紛争防止、村領土完全性の集団責任を保証。

items.title_ja: 開帳の系図記録維持
items.desc_en: Specialized practice of maintaining and updating kaicho genealogical records documenting munchuu lineage across generations. Includes verification of adoptions, marriages, and death records through community testimony and oral history validation methods.
items.desc_ja: 世代を超えた門中の系図を記録する開帳の管理実践。養子入り、婚姻、死亡記録の検証を含む。

items.title_ja: 模合投資プール形成
items.desc_en: Financial cooperative system where community members contribute regular funds for capital projects like shop establishment or home renovation. Rotation determines benefit recipients, requiring trust-based record keeping and transparent allocation decisions.
items.desc_ja: 商店設立や住宅改修のため定期的に資金を拠出する協同システム。信頼に基づく記録管理が必要。

items.title_ja: 口引き村集会儀礼
items.desc_en: Traditional village meeting format where specific individuals speak on behalf of community sections, ensuring all voices integrate into consensus decisions. Protocol includes prescribed speaking order, gesture conventions, and conflict resolution through graduated intervention.
items.desc_ja: 村落区域の代表が話す集会儀式。段階的な仲裁と合意形成の慣行。

items.title_ja: 養子組み採用式統合
items.desc_en: Formal procedures integrating adopted children into munchuu through ritual recognition by elders, name registration in genealogy books, and community acknowledgment ceremonies. Establishes inheritance rights and social status within family and wider kinship networks.
items.desc_ja: 養子を門中に儀式的に統合する手続き。名前登録と社会的地位確立を含む。

items.title_ja: 洗骨儀式知識
items.desc_en: Specialized mortuary practice involving ceremonial washing and handling of ancestral bones during transitional reburial, typically performed by designated family members or specialists. Requires knowledge of proper sequence, prayers, and spiritual preparations.
items.desc_ja: 埋葬時の祖先の骨の儀式的洗浄。特定の家族成員または専門家による実行。

items.title_ja: 三三九度杯交換儀礼
items.desc_en: Complex three-stage sake cup exchange ceremony establishing marital bonds between individuals and families. Involves prescribed pouring sequences, cup presentation etiquette, and symbolic meanings tied to fertility and household unity.
items.desc_ja: 三段階の盃交換で婚姻関係を確立。注ぎの順序と象徴的意味を含む。

items.title_ja: 若者組み年齢段階組織
items.desc_en: Hierarchical age-based youth groups managing community labor projects, festival preparations, and martial training. Members progress through ranks with increasing responsibilities, maintaining village security and coordinating collective agricultural work seasons.
items.desc_ja: 年齢ベースの若者グループ。祭礼準備と農業労働を管理。階級制度を有する。

items.title_ja: 親方村落指導者権限委譲
items.desc_en: Traditional system of delegated authority where selected umui headmen coordinate community functions through subordinate officials. Includes decision-making hierarchies, resource allocation authority, and dispute arbitration responsibilities within defined village territories.
items.desc_ja: 選出された親方が部下の役人を通じて権限を行使。資源配分と紛争仲裁を含む。

items.title_ja: 飲酒模合社交債務維持
items.desc_en: Rotating social drinking groups establishing mutual obligation networks through regular meetings and shared expenses. Participants take turns hosting, with structured socializing patterns that reinforce community bonds and economic interdependence.
items.desc_ja: 定期的な飲酒会で相互扶助ネットワークを構築。ホスト交代制で相互債務を強化。

items.title_en: Seasonal Sugar Cane Harvest Labor Cooperation
items.title_ja: 季節的サトウキビ収穫労働協力
items.desc_en: Coordinated community labor system organizing workers through munchuu networks for simultaneous sugar cane harvesting. Involves task allocation by age and capability, shared tool use protocols, and collective processing arrangements benefiting participating families.
items.desc_ja: 門中ネットワークで計画された共同収穫労働。年齢別の役割配置と共有道具利用。

items.title_en: Kaicho Genealogy Book Maintenance and Succession
items.title_ja: 家譜(かいちょ)の保管と世代継承
items.desc_en: Specialized practice of maintaining detailed genealogy records documenting munchuu lineage, births, marriages, and adoptions. Kaicho keepers preserve historical family connections and settle inheritance disputes through documented genealogical evidence.
items.desc_ja: 門中の系統を記録した家譜を世代ごとに正確に保管・更新する知識体系。血縁関係の証明や相続問題の解決に活用される。

items.title_ja: 養子組(ようしぐみ)の門中編入儀礼
items.desc_en: Formal procedures integrating adopted children into munchuu membership through ritual acknowledgment and genealogical registration. Establishes adopted child's legal and social standing within extended family networks and inheritance hierarchies.
items.desc_ja: 養子を正式に門中に受け入れるための儀礼と手続き。系図への記載と社会的地位確認。

items.title_ja: 十七日忌(じゅうしちにちき)の忌明け儀礼
items.desc_en: Ritual calendar marking deceased's spiritual transition through specific ceremonies on the forty-ninth day after death. Coordinates community participation, ancestor veneration, and formal mourning period closure through prescribed observances.
items.desc_ja: 故人が極楽往生するまでの49日間の儀礼体系。特定の日に行う供養と家族の服喪の終了。

items.title_ja: 洗骨(せんこつ)の儀式知識
items.desc_en: Specialized knowledge of bone-washing ceremonies involving careful cleaning and rearrangement of ancestral remains. Requires understanding of proper tools, timing, family participation protocols, and spiritual preparation rituals before reburial.
items.desc_ja: 墓から遺骨を取り出して洗浄し、甕に収納する儀式知識。特定の道具と作法が必要。

items.title_ja: 口引き(くちひき)の村落会議
items.desc_en: Traditional village assembly process where household representatives discuss communal issues through consensus-building dialogue. Leaders (umui) facilitate discussion using rhetorical techniques to achieve unified village decisions on shared resources and obligations.
items.desc_ja: 村落の首長が主導する全戸代表による会議。合意形成を通じた村の意思決定。

items.title_ja: 講(こう)の投資型相互扶助
items.desc_en: Rotating savings and credit association where members pool capital for business ventures or land purchases. Requires trust-based financial management, transparent record-keeping, and penalties for non-compliance enforced through social reputation mechanisms.
items.desc_ja: 複数の成員が資金を拠出・分配する相互扶助組織。事業投資や土地購入に活用。

items.title_ja: 若者組(わかものぐみ)の同年代組織
items.desc_en: Age-graded youth associations organizing communal labor, village festivals, and social activities. Youth progress through hierarchical roles linked to age cohorts, teaching cooperation, leadership skills, and village responsibility through active participation.
items.desc_ja: 同年代の若者で構成される組織。共同労働や祭礼の主要な担い手。

items.title_ja: 結納(ゆいの)の婚約儀礼
items.desc_en: Formalized betrothal gift exchange ceremony establishing marriage contract between families. Specifies symbolic gift types, presentation order, negotiation procedures, and family role assignments differing from Japanese mainland practices through regional adaptations.
items.desc_ja: 婚約を結ぶ際の両家間の儀礼的贈答。沖縄独自の贈り物と儀式の順序。

items.title_ja: 三三九度(さんさんくど)の結婚儀式
items.desc_en: Three-times-three sake cup exchange ceremony solemnizing marriage vows. Involves precise hand movements, cup sizes, and sake quality specifications. Okinawan variant emphasizes community witness and ritual purity elements distinct from mainland Japanese versions.
items.desc_ja: 新郎新婦が三度ずつ盃を交わす結婚式。沖縄独自の作法と社会的意味。

items.title_en: Sugar Cane Harvest Seasonal Labor Coordination
items.title_ja: さとうきび収穫の季節的協力労働
items.desc_en: Organized communal labor system coordinating village-wide sugar cane harvesting through task division and rotation. Involves negotiating work schedules, managing shared tools, distributing harvest shares equitably, and reinforcing social bonds through collective agricultural effort.
items.desc_ja: 砂糖きび収穫期の村落全体による共同労働。作業分担と報酬配分の仕組み。

items.title_en: Kaicho Genealogy Documentation and Munchuu Record-Keeping
items.title_ja: 開帳と門中記録の管理
items.desc_en: Systematic documentation of family genealogies in kaicho books, maintaining munchuu membership records, tracking adoptions, and preserving ancestor lineages. Records serve legal and ceremonial purposes, with designated keepers responsible for accuracy and accessibility.
items.desc_ja: 開帳に記された家系図の管理、門中の構成員記録、養子関係の追跡、先祖系統の保存。法律および儀式目的で使用され、指定された記録管理者が正確性と利用可能性を確保。

items.title_en: Senkotsu Bone-Washing Ritual and Ancestral Veneration
items.title_ja: 洗骨と祖先崇拝の儀式
items.desc_en: Ritual practice of exhuming and ceremonially washing ancestor bones, typically occurring several years after burial. Involves family participation, specific prayers, and reinterment in family tombs, strengthening bonds between living and deceased munchuu members.
items.desc_ja: 埋葬後数年経過後に祖先の遺骨を掘り起こし儀式的に洗浄する実践。家族の参加、特定の祈り、家系の墓への再埋葬を含み、生者と故人の門中間の絆を強化。

items.title_en: Yushi-Gumi Adoption Protocol and Munchuu Integration
items.title_ja: 養子組の統合と門中編入
items.desc_en: Formal procedures for adopting children into munchuu, establishing legal kinship, inheritance rights, and ceremonial participation. Includes naming ceremonies, genealogical registration, and elaborate rituals recognizing the adopted child's new family status.
items.desc_ja: 養子を門中に正式に受け入れる手順で、法的親族関係、相続権、儀式への参加を確立。命名式、系図登録、養子の新しい家族地位を認める儀式を含む。

items.title_en: Drinking Moai: Seasonal Gathering and Reciprocal Social Bonds
items.title_ja: 飲み相互扶助の季節集会
items.desc_en: Organized drinking groups meeting regularly to strengthen community bonds, celebrate seasonal events, and provide mutual emotional support. Members contribute equally to expenses, reinforcing reciprocal obligation and social cohesion across generational and occupational lines.
items.desc_ja: 定期的に集会するアルコール消費グループで、コミュニティの絆強化、季節行事の祝い、相互情動的支援を提供。成員は費用に同等に寄与し、相互義務と社会的結束を強化。

items.title_en: Travel Moai Cooperative Journey Groups and Collective Pilgrimage
items.title_ja: 旅行相互扶助グループと共同巡礼
items.desc_en: Organized groups pooling resources for collective travel, pilgrimage to sacred sites, visiting relatives, or seasonal migrations. Members share transportation, accommodation, and expenses while fulfilling spiritual obligations and maintaining extended family networks across islands.
items.desc_ja: 共同旅行、聖地への巡礼、親戚訪問、季節的移動のため資源をプール。成員は交通、宿泊、費用を共有しながら精神的義務を果たし、島々間の拡大家族ネットワークを維持。

items.title_en: Kuchihiki Village Assembly: Consensus Decision-Making Protocol
items.title_ja: 口引きと村落集会の合意形成
items.desc_en: Traditional village assembly where community members gather to discuss and collectively decide matters affecting village welfare. Emphasizes consensus-building, elder mediation, and oral transmission of decisions through designated speakers representing neighborhood clusters.
items.desc_ja: 村落の福利に関わる事項について議論・決定するため村人が集会。合意形成、高齢者調停、近隣クラスター代表による決定の口頭伝達を強調。

items.title_en: San-San-Kudo Wedding Exchange: Symbolic Cup-Sharing Ritual
items.title_ja: 三三九度の結婚儀式
items.desc_en: Okinawan wedding ceremony where bride and groom exchange drinks from three cups in nine rounds, symbolizing union, commitment, and family integration. Reflects local variations of mainland practice, incorporating island-specific blessings and ancestral acknowledgment.
items.desc_ja: 新郎新婦が三つの杯から九回飲み交わす結婚式で、結合、誓約、家族統合を象徴。本土慣行の地域変種で、島固有の祝福と先祖承認を組入。

items.title_en: Investment Moai and Rotating Savings Cooperative Finance
items.title_ja: 投資相互扶助と輪番貯蓄協同組合
items.desc_en: Formalized rotating savings group where members contribute fixed amounts monthly, with accumulated funds distributed sequentially to each member. Enables capital accumulation for major investments like house construction, agricultural equipment, or business ventures without institutional credit.
items.desc_ja: 成員が毎月固定額を寄与し、累積資金を順次各成員に配分する輪番貯蓄グループ。制度的信用なしで家屋建設、農業機械、事業投資のため資本蓄積を可能。

items.title_en: Wakamonogumi Youth Age-Grade Hierarchies and Collective Responsibility
items.title_ja: 若者組と年齢階級制
items.desc_en: Structured youth organizations dividing young men by age cohort with progressive responsibilities for village maintenance, festival organization, and collective labor. Members advance through ranks, learning community obligations while participating in same-age peer solidarity networks.
items.desc_ja: 年齢コホート別に若年男性を分け、村落維持、祭り組織、集団労働に段階的責任を割当。成員は階級を通じ進行し、同年代同輩連帯ネットワーク参加時に共同体義務を学習。

items.title_en: Communal Land Tenure System and Seasonal Harvest Cooperation
items.title_ja: 共有地制度と季節的収穫協力
items.desc_en: Traditional system allocating village communal lands for collective sugar cane harvests, vegetable cultivation, and grazing. Families rotate usufruct rights seasonally, coordinate planting schedules, and participate in mandatory communal labor exchanges during peak agricultural periods.
items.desc_ja: 村落共有地をサトウキビ収穫、野菜栽培、放牧に配分する制度。家族が季節的用益権を交替し、植付スケジュール調整、農業ピーク時の強制的共同労働交換に参加。

items.title_ja: 海帳検証儀式
items.desc_en: Formal ritual procedures for authenticating genealogical records in kaicho books, involving elder testimony, documentary evidence presentation, and munchuu council verification to maintain ancestral legitimacy and inheritance rights within family lineages.
items.desc_ja: 家譜の正当性を確認する儀式的手続き。長老の証言、文書提示、門中評議会による検証を通じて系統の継続性を保証する。

items.title_en: Tomb Record Maintenance and Bone Classification
items.title_ja: 墓記録と骨分類の維持
items.desc_en: Specialized knowledge of organizing skeletal remains in family tombs, including bone classification systems, genealogical mapping within tombs, and maintenance protocols ensuring proper ancestral placement and identification across generations.
items.desc_ja: 家族の墓内における骨格遺骨の管理体系。分類システム、墓内系統図作成、世代を超えた正確な先祖特定と配置を確保する維持管理手法。

items.title_en: Seasonal Investment Moai Formation and Management
items.title_ja: 季節投資モアイの形成と管理
items.desc_en: Structured mutual aid groups focused on seasonal agricultural investments, coordinating capital pooling for equipment purchases, seed acquisition, and labor hiring during peak farming periods with rotating financial responsibility among members.
items.desc_ja: 農業投資に特化した季節的相互扶助組織。設備購入、種子取得、農繁期の雇用労働に資金を集約し、メンバー間で回転的に財務責任を分担する仕組み。

items.title_en: Women's Ceremonial Moai and Ritual Leadership
items.title_ja: 女性儀式モアイと儀式指導権
items.desc_en: Exclusive female mutual aid associations organizing shrine maintenance, lifecycle rituals, communal cooking ceremonies, and spiritual observances with internal hierarchies determining ritual participation rights and ceremonial leadership roles.
items.desc_ja: 女性専属の相互扶助組織。神社維持、生涯儀式、共同調理式、精神的儀式を組織し、内部階層による儀式参加権と指導役割を決定する。

items.title_ja: 口引き共同体意思決定プロトコル
items.desc_en: Formal rhetorical procedures in village assemblies where designated speakers articulate collective concerns through stylized speech patterns, consensus-building dialogue, and hierarchical opinion presentation ensuring elder voices guide community resolution processes.
items.desc_ja: 村落集会における形式的修辞手法。指定スピーカーが様式化された言語パターンで集団的関心を表現し、合意形成対話と階層的意見提示を通じて長老の声を優先させる。

items.title_en: Umui Headman Authority and Village Welfare Obligations
items.title_ja: 村長権限と村落福祉義務
items.desc_en: Detailed knowledge of village headman responsibilities including dispute arbitration, resource distribution oversight, communal labor coordination, and welfare provision for disadvantaged members within shima territories and munchuu networks.
items.desc_ja: 村落指導者の責任体系。紛争仲裁、資源配分監督、共同労働調整、不利立場にある成員への福祉提供が島と門中ネットワーク内で機能する方法。

items.title_en: Yushi-gumi Adoption Integration Rituals and Munchuu Entry
items.title_ja: 養子統合儀式と門中編入
items.desc_en: Ceremonial procedures for integrating adopted children into munchuu lineages, including name registrations, genealogical positioning, inheritance right negotiations, and symbolic bonding rituals affirming adopted status within family hierarchies.
items.desc_ja: 養子を門中系統に統合する儀式的手続き。命名登録、系統図上の位置づけ、相続権交渉、採用地位を確認する象徴的結合儀式により家族階層での組み込みを実現。

items.title_ja: 四十九日死後期間管理
items.desc_en: Extended ritual knowledge governing the 49-day period following death, including daily offerings, prayers, spirit transition beliefs, household purification protocols, and culminating ceremonies that mark deceased transition to ancestral status.
items.desc_ja: 死後49日間を統制する儀式知識体系。日々の供物、祈祷、霊の移行信仰、家族浄化プロトコル、先祖地位への移行を標示する最終儀式を包含する。

items.title_ja: 若ものぐみ年齢階級進級儀式
items.desc_en: Structured rite-of-passage ceremonies marking progression through male youth grades, including strength competitions, communal feast participation, leadership role assumption, and formal recognition by village elders within age-cohort hierarchies.
items.desc_ja: 男性青年階級の進級を標示する段階的通過儀礼。体力競技、共同食事参加、指導役就任、年齢同輩団階層内での長老による正式認定により達成される。

items.title_en: Communal Land Tenure Rotation and Seasonal Redistribution
items.title_ja: 共有地占有ローテーションと季節的再配分
items.desc_en: Traditional land management systems allocating agricultural fields through rotating tenancy periods, ensuring equitable access to productive land among munchuu members while maintaining collective ownership and preventing accumulation disparities.
items.desc_ja: 農業用地を回転的借地期間により配分する従来的土地管理体系。門中成員間での衡平な生産地へのアクセスを保証しつつ、集団的所有権を維持し蓄積格差を防ぐ。

items.title_en: Kaicho Genealogy Book Maintenance and Verification
items.title_ja: 開帳の系図検証と維持管理
items.desc_en: Specialized practice of maintaining kaicho genealogy books, verifying lineage accuracy through documentary evidence, birth records, and tomb inscriptions. Practitioners cross-reference multiple sources to prevent genealogical errors and ensure munchuu membership legitimacy across generations.
items.desc_ja: 開帳の系図を維持管理し、文書証拠、埋葬記録、墓碑銘を通じて血統の正確性を検証する専門的実践。複数の資料を相互参照して系図の誤りを防止し、門中の構成員資格の正当性を保証する。

items.title_en: Senkotsu Ritual Bone Washing Sequence and Timing
items.title_ja: 洗骨儀式の手順と時間経過
items.desc_en: Prescribed ritual procedures for washing and purifying ancestral bones during secondary burial. Specialists determine precise timing based on death season, perform ceremonial washing in specific order, and coordinate with lunar calendar phases for proper ancestral veneration.
items.desc_ja: 二次埋葬時に祖先の遺骨を洗浄・浄化するための規定された儀式手順。死亡季節に基づいて正確なタイミングを決定し、規定の順序で儀式的洗浄を実行し、旧暦の段階と調整する。

items.title_en: Yushi-gumi Adoption Integration into Munchuu Structure
items.title_ja: 養子組の門中統合プロセス
items.desc_en: Complex procedures integrating adopted children into munchuu lineage systems. Involves formal ceremonies, name-changing rituals, genealogical registration, and establishment of succession rights within extended family structures, ensuring full social recognition and inheritance eligibility.
items.desc_ja: 養子を門中の血統体系に統合する複雑な手続き。正式な儀式、改名儀礼、系図登録、拡大家族内の相続権の確立を含み、完全な社会的認識と相続適格性を保証する。

items.title_en: Investment Moai Financial Management and Profit Distribution
items.title_ja: 投資模合の資金管理と利益配分
items.desc_en: Specialized financial cooperative rotating system where members pool capital for business ventures or land purchases. Managers calculate returns, distribute profits according to traditional formulas, and maintain detailed accounting records to ensure fair allocation among rotating members.
items.desc_ja: メンバーが事業や土地購入のために資本をプールする回転制の金融協力システム。管理者が利益を計算し、伝統的公式に従って配分し、詳細な会計記録を維持して回転メンバー間の公正な配分を確保する。

items.title_ja: 口引きの村落集会決定プロトコル
items.desc_en: Formal decision-making procedures in village assemblies where community consensus is built through sequential speaker turns and structured debate. Elders facilitate discussion ensuring all viewpoints heard before consensus resolution, maintaining social harmony while addressing disputes.
items.desc_ja: 逐次的な発話順序と構造化された議論を通じて地域の合意を構築する村落集会の正式な意思決定手順。長老が議論を主導し、合意解決前にすべての見方が聞かれることを確保し、社会的調和を維持しながら紛争に対処する。

items.title_en: San-san-kudo Ceremonial Cup Exchange Ritual Variations
items.title_ja: 三三九度の儀式的杯交換の地域差
items.desc_en: Wedding ceremony variations of san-san-kudo cup exchange ritual across different Okinawan regions. Specialists recognize regional differences in cup number sequences, drink types, sacred words, and family participation patterns, adapting ceremonies to local munchuu traditions.
items.desc_ja: 沖縄の異なる地域間における三三九度の杯交換儀式の結婚式の変動。専門家は杯数列、飲料の種類、神聖な言葉、家族参加パターンの地域差を認識し、地元門中伝統に適応させて式典を調整する。

items.title_en: Umui Headman Authority and Community Responsibility Balance
items.title_ja: 親方の権限と地域責任のバランス
items.desc_en: Traditional village headman role balancing personal authority with communal obligations. Umui adjudicates disputes, organizes collective labor, allocates communal resources, and represents village interests while maintaining accountability through elder council oversight and community consensus requ
items.desc_ja: 個人的権限と共有の義務を調整する伝統的な村の長の役割。親方は紛争を裁定し、集団労働を組織し、共有資源を配分し、長老会の監督と地域合意要件を通じて説明責任を維持しながら村の利益を代表する。

items.title_en: Wakamonogumi Youth Grade Progression and Responsibility Transfer
items.title_ja: 若者組の年級進行と責任移譲
items.desc_en: Age-grade system structuring youth group progression from entry to leadership roles. Each grade level assigns specific community duties, ritual responsibilities, and ceremonial leadership opportunities. Advancement follows prescribed age milestones, creating structured pathway toward adult community
items.desc_ja: 入会から指導的役割への若者グループの進行を構造化する年級システム。各段階は特定の地域の義務、儀式の責任、儀式的指導の機会を割り当てる。進行は規定の年齢マイルストーンに従い、成人の地域参加への構造化された道を作成する。

items.title_en: Communal Land Tenure Recognition and Seasonal Access Rights
items.title_ja: 共有地の認識と季節別アクセス権
items.desc_en: Traditional systems recognizing communal land ownership with seasonal harvesting and grazing rights. Communities maintain collective memory of boundary markers, seasonal access schedules, and usufruct patterns. Elders arbitrate disputes through oral testimony and customary precedent without formal d
items.desc_ja: 季節的採集と放牧権を伴う共有地所有権を認識する伝統的体系。コミュニティは境界標識、季節別アクセススケジュール、慣習的利用パターンの集団的記憶を維持する。長老は正式な文書化なしに口頭証拠と慣習的先例を通じて紛争を仲裁する。

items.title_ja: 一周忌の一年間の追悼スケジューリング
items.desc_en: Detailed practice organizing first-year memorial ceremonies following prescribed lunar calendar intervals. Families coordinate multiple ritual observances at specific dates, invite munchuu members, arrange offerings, and document attendance. Specialists calculate proper timing ensuring spiritual con
items.desc_ja: 規定の旧暦間隔に従う一年の追悼儀式を組織する詳細な実践。家族は特定の日付で複数の儀式的遵守を調整し、門中メンバーを招待し、供物を準備し、出席を記録する。専門家は故人との霊的連続性を確保する適切なタイミングを計算する。
